jokes.text: I'm not reading that! Summarize it in one word. Sabotage.
jokes.setup: Hermes refuses to read a detailed crash report and demands a one-word summary
jokes.punchline: The professor delivers the absurdly dramatic 'Sabotage' as if it's a mundane summary
jokes.explanation: Strong economy and character work - typical bureaucratic laziness meets dramatic revelation

jokes.text: That's not an L! Unless you count lowercase.
jokes.setup: Looking at a bent L-shaped component that no longer looks like an L
jokes.punchline: Pointing out it resembles a lowercase 'l' instead
jokes.explanation: Simple visual pun, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You know we don't!
jokes.setup: Amy's joke about counting lowercase letters
jokes.punchline: Leela's serious response treating it as an official policy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurdity - treating nonsense as bureaucratic rule

jokes.text: Sweet lamprey of Santa Fe! My edge has been bent!
jokes.setup: Professor needs to draw a straight line with his straightedge
jokes.punchline: His measuring tool has been bent, plus absurd exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor exclamation plus wordplay on 'edge' being bent - solid character moment

jokes.text: My javelin too! It's ruined! Now we'll never beat Jupiter State!
jokes.setup: Everything is being bent, including sports equipment
jokes.punchline: Treating javelin bending as a catastrophic loss to their athletic program
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the bending problem to ridiculous consequences

jokes.text: Thank you for your sympathy, talking square of linoleum.
jokes.setup: Professor has been bent and someone expresses sympathy
jokes.punchline: He refers to the speaker as a 'talking square of linoleum' in a formal, polite manner
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor Farnsworth line - perfectly captures his mix of politeness and oblivious insults

jokes.text: A bunch of stuff gets bent, so it must be the robot designed for bending.
jokes.setup: Everyone immediately suspects Bender
jokes.punchline: Fry sarcastically points out how obvious and unfair this assumption is
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about prejudice, but fairly straightforward

jokes.text: You've seen me naked?
jokes.setup: Discussion about surveillance tapes to solve the mystery
jokes.punchline: Bender's only concern is his privacy/vanity about being seen naked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender misdirection - focuses on vanity instead of guilt

jokes.text: It was right next to Bathroom Bloopers Four.
jokes.setup: Finding the surveillance tape
jokes.punchline: Implies there's a whole series of bathroom blooper tapes
jokes.explanation: Good world-building joke about their weird video collection

jokes.text: Wait, there on the screen! It's that guy you are!
jokes.setup: Looking at surveillance footage of Bender
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward phrasing 'that guy you are' instead of 'you'
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry awkward phrasing - good character work but minor joke

jokes.text: We've got robots on the ceiling.
jokes.setup: Watching surveillance footage of sleepwalking Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender is apparently walking on the ceiling while sleep-bending
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag - absurd image of robot sleepwalking on ceiling

jokes.text: Mama.
jokes.setup: Bender's birth flashback on an assembly line
jokes.punchline: Newborn robot Bender calling the assembly line machine 'Mama'
jokes.explanation: Perfect single-word joke combining robot nature with universal baby experience

jokes.text: Precocious little scamp, ain't I?
jokes.setup: Revelation that Bender is only 4 years old
jokes.punchline: Bender referring to himself with terms typically used by adults about children
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing with age expectations

jokes.text: He's a menace to every straight person in the company.
jokes.setup: Discussing Bender's bending problem
jokes.punchline: 'Straight' meaning both unbent objects and heterosexual people
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning of 'straight' - works on multiple levels

jokes.text: No more bending, no more work Give us a raise, you big fat jerk
jokes.setup: Labor strike chant
jokes.punchline: Childish name-calling ('big fat jerk') in what should be a serious labor protest
jokes.explanation: Decent contrast between serious labor issues and childish insults

jokes.text: Yo! The mafia supports you! But don't tell no one! Spread the word!
jokes.setup: Mafia showing support for the strike
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instructions to both keep it secret AND spread the word
jokes.explanation: Excellent contradiction joke - perfectly captures confused criminal logic

jokes.text: What if management is 'intragnizent'? In context, it's clear what you mean.
jokes.setup: Don-bot trying to say 'intransigent' but mispronouncing it
jokes.punchline: Clamps politely covering for the malapropism
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism with polite correction - nice character dynamic

jokes.text: In that case, Clamps may have a surprise for them. The clamps! Right?
jokes.setup: Threatening management if they don't cooperate
jokes.punchline: Clamps' signature threat followed by seeking approval
jokes.explanation: Classic Clamps character moment - threatening but insecure

jokes.text: They hire scabs at 10 times our wage!
jokes.setup: Complaining about scab workers
jokes.punchline: The scabs are being paid 10 times MORE than the regular workers
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of expected labor economics - makes the strike seem pointless

jokes.text: Sorry you got sent to that South American Turkish prison instead of me.
jokes.setup: Flexo and Bender reunion
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to bizarrely specific prison location that makes no geographic sense
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail - 'South American Turkish prison' is wonderfully nonsensical

jokes.text: You bastard! They treated me like an animal! And that's what I became!
jokes.setup: Bender's angry about the prison experience
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration that he 'became an animal' - which is absurd for a robot
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - robot talking about becoming an animal is inherently absurd

jokes.text: No, you're all right. Good to see you!
jokes.setup: Bender just had an angry outburst about prison
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to friendly casual greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender emotional whiplash - dramatic to casual instantly

jokes.text: Hello. Hey, you guys want to move that things?
jokes.setup: Angleene greeting the group
jokes.punchline: Presumably Bender is staring/frozen, needs to be moved like an object
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag inference - Bender probably frozen with attraction

jokes.text: Hey, according to the Scab Handbook, that's inappropriate banter.
jokes.setup: Bender making sexual innuendo about her access panel
jokes.punchline: Reference to an actual 'Scab Handbook' with workplace conduct rules
jokes.explanation: Great world-building joke - the idea of a handbook for replacement workers

jokes.text: Haile H. Selassie!
jokes.setup: Professor expressing surprise or amazement
jokes.punchline: Using Ethiopian emperor as an exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth historical figure exclamation - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Those delightful birds with their chirp, chirp and their tweet, tweet, splat.
jokes.setup: Professor appreciating nature's beauty and bird sounds
jokes.punchline: Adding 'splat' suggests the birds are hitting something/dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from peaceful nature sounds to implied death

jokes.text: I wish. It's a nickel.
jokes.setup: Professor says Fry has a dime up his nose
jokes.punchline: Fry's disappointed correction that it's only a nickel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - disappointed about having less valuable nose-money

jokes.text: My legs are made of rubber.
jokes.setup: Bender saying watching him bend turns legs rubbery
jokes.punchline: Angleene's matter-of-fact correction that her legs are literally rubber
jokes.explanation: Good robot literalism - takes metaphor literally because it's actually true

jokes.text: Some say I'm robbing the cradle, but I say she's robbing the grave.
jokes.setup: Professor talking about dating a much younger woman
jokes.punchline: Clever reversal suggesting he's so old he's practically dead
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal of the 'robbing the cradle' phrase - clever and character-specific

jokes.text: It's got a nice profit margin. Bam!
jokes.setup: Elzar recommending the pasta
jokes.punchline: Recommending food based on profit margin rather than taste, plus signature 'Bam!'
jokes.explanation: Good Elzar character moment - mercenary chef with cooking show catchphrase

jokes.text: To celebrate, I'm gonna stick seven copies of my book on your bill.
jokes.setup: Elzar being congratulatory about Bender's news
jokes.punchline: His 'celebration' is charging them for unwanted books
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Elzar's mercenary nature - turns celebration into sales opportunity

jokes.text: Careful, that's a week old.
jokes.setup: Bender presumably throws something in anger
jokes.punchline: Casual warning about the age of whatever Bender threw
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag timing - casual reaction to dramatic moment

jokes.text: You degenerate hussy! I'm disappointed in you too, Angleene!
jokes.setup: Bender discovering Angleene with Flexo
jokes.punchline: Calls Flexo a 'degenerate hussy' then addresses Angleene as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - calling the male robot a 'hussy' while treating his girlfriend as secondary. The casual 'I'm disappointed in you too' is perfect.

jokes.text: God, then it's worse than I think!
jokes.setup: Angleene says 'it's not what you think'
jokes.punchline: Bender assumes this means it's somehow worse
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid logic from Bender - perfect character response

jokes.text: What I'm outraged by is your choice of wine!
jokes.setup: Bender learning they're just divorced friends having dinner
jokes.punchline: Switches to criticizing wine choice instead of admitting he overreacted
jokes.explanation: Perfect face-saving deflection from Bender - maintains his ego while backing down

jokes.text: Either that, or be a man.
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting Bender talk honestly about his feelings
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggesting the opposite (deception) as being 'manly'
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic commentary on toxic masculinity

jokes.text: show up as him and catch her two-timing me with myself.
jokes.setup: Bender's elaborate deception plan
jokes.punchline: The logical contradiction of catching someone cheating 'with myself'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - the perfect culmination of Bender's convoluted jealousy plan

jokes.text: Flexo, the fun-loving love machine fembots love to love.
jokes.setup: Bender pretending to be Flexo
jokes.punchline: Overwrought alliterative self-description that's obviously fake
jokes.explanation: Good over-the-top fake persona - shows Bender's poor acting skills

jokes.text: I'll have a fuzzy navel. She'll have the girliest drink in the house.
jokes.setup: Bender pretending to be Flexo on date with Angleene
jokes.punchline: Orders fuzzy navel (girly drink) then asks for even girlier drink for her
jokes.explanation: Good irony - orders famously girly drink then asks for something even girlier. Shows Bender doesn't really understand what makes Flexo different.

jokes.text: I hope they can change a fortune.
jokes.setup: Making fuzzy navel drinks
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'change a fortune' instead of standard phrase
jokes.explanation: Minor malapropism - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: That cash should be slushing my fund and kicks-ing my back.
jokes.setup: Clamps angry about scab's money
jokes.punchline: Mangled phrases mixing 'slush fund' and 'kickback'
jokes.explanation: Good Clamps malapropism - mixes up criminal terminology appropriately for character

jokes.text: I'm greasing up my whoozits!
jokes.setup: Clamps getting ready for violence
jokes.punchline: Uses childish euphemism 'whoozits' for his clamps/weapons
jokes.explanation: Good Clamps character moment. Using cutesy language for violence preparation fits his oddball personality.

jokes.text: Bender? That walking wuss factory?
jokes.setup: Angleene says she loves Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender (disguised as Flexo) insulting himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Bender forced to insult himself while in disguise

jokes.text: He may be a walking factory, but he is no wuss.
jokes.setup: Defending Bender against 'Flexo's' insult
jokes.punchline: Accepts the 'walking factory' part but defends against 'wuss'
jokes.explanation: Good selective defense - accepts one insult but rejects the other

jokes.text: I recently upgraded my funk card.
jokes.setup: Angleene notes that 'Flexo' has loosened up and become more flexible
jokes.punchline: Bender's ridiculous explanation involving a 'funk card'
jokes.explanation: Good absurd tech explanation - 'funk card' is wonderfully nonsensical

jokes.text: The moral of the story is: If you want it to stay sunk, tie a weight to it!
jokes.setup: Don-bot apparently telling a story
jokes.punchline: The 'moral' is clearly about disposing of bodies/evidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - presents criminal advice as wholesome moral lesson

jokes.text: Aw, shucks, thunder-buns. You make me feel like a million volts.
jokes.setup: Romantic conversation
jokes.punchline: Robot-appropriate romantic language using electrical terms
jokes.explanation: Good robot romance language - 'million volts' instead of million bucks

jokes.text: You always were a kick in the teeth, man. / Aw, shucks, thunder-buns. You make me feel like a million volts.
jokes.setup: Romantic compliment about being exciting
jokes.punchline: Bender's electrical metaphor for arousal
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific romantic language with electrical metaphors.

jokes.text: Don-bot, I don't think we should rely on an accident happening.
jokes.setup: Don-bot saying the scab will have an 'accident'
jokes.punchline: Clamps being overly literal about the euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Clamps missing the euphemistic meaning

jokes.text: Maybe I love you no matter who you're pretending to be!
jokes.setup: Bender revealed his deception
jokes.punchline: Angleene's romantic declaration that actually makes the deception pointless
jokes.explanation: Excellent romantic irony - her love makes his elaborate scheme unnecessary

jokes.text: Oh, how I wish I could believe or understand that!
jokes.setup: Angleene's declaration of unconditional love
jokes.punchline: Bender admits he can't process genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - emotionally stunted robot unable to process love

jokes.text: Thanks, I appreciate that. Nah, I'm joshing you.
jokes.setup: Flexo apparently thanking someone for help
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he was joking about the appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good Flexo character moment - casual dismissal of gratitude

jokes.text: Give the word and I'll drop this unbendable girder. Clamp-ka-bamp!
jokes.setup: Clamps threatening to drop a girder on someone
jokes.punchline: Adding his signature sound effect 'Clamp-ka-bamp!'
jokes.explanation: Good Clamps catchphrase moment - sound effect for dropping heavy things

jokes.text: Oh, that's gotta clamp.
jokes.setup: Heavy girder falling on someone
jokes.punchline: Clamps using 'clamp' as a verb meaning 'hurt'
jokes.explanation: Good character wordplay - using his signature tool as slang

jokes.text: I'm yanking your chain. I'm dying.
jokes.setup: Flexo says he's 'never better'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was joking and is actually dying
jokes.explanation: Good dark misdirection - casual joking about serious injury

jokes.text: I'm yanking your chain. I'm dying.
jokes.setup: Flexo says he's never better after injury
jokes.punchline: Admits he was joking and is actually dying
jokes.explanation: Classic Flexo - can't even be sincere when dying. The casual delivery of mortal information is both dark and funny.

jokes.text: But I could never love anyone as much as you made me realize I love Flexo!
jokes.setup: Romantic triangle resolution
jokes.punchline: Angleene thanks Bender for making her realize she loves someone else
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic resolution - Bender's scheme backfires completely

jokes.text: I don't know anything about lifting, so that just leaves us the one option.
jokes.setup: Trying to save Flexo from under an unbendable girder
jokes.punchline: Admitting ignorance about lifting implies he'll try bending instead
jokes.explanation: Good setup for heroic bending moment - Bender logic at work

jokes.text: Me too. Me too. Jerk.
jokes.setup: Touching moment where Bender saves Flexo and they bond
jokes.punchline: Bender adds 'Jerk' under his breath, unable to maintain sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - can't resist undermining his own emotional growth

jokes.text: a bend to a place where happiness is perpendicular to wonderment.
jokes.setup: Professor giving philosophical advice about outlook
jokes.punchline: Using geometric terms for abstract emotional concepts
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor pseudo-scientific wisdom using mathematical terms for feelings

jokes.text: We all need a new angle on life
jokes.setup: Professor singing about life philosophy
jokes.punchline: Uses 'angle' as pun since he's been bent at odd angle
jokes.explanation: Solid pun tying together the episode's bending theme with life philosophy. Not exceptional but well-integrated with the plot.

jokes.text: I try to get out, but they keep pulling me back in!
jokes.setup: Crew trying to bend Professor back to normal
jokes.punchline: Classic Godfather III quote about trying to escape the mafia
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits the situation. Professor trying to escape being bent back parallels Michael Corleone trying to escape the mob.

jokes.text: I'm sad now. It's fine.
jokes.setup: Professor gets bent back to his normal pessimistic position
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acceptance of returning to depression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - casual acceptance of emotional state change
